title: Add pictures/ themes to dashboardsda

posts.author: carcher85 (Claire Archer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Add images to your dashboards like you can add videos etc.

Update background themes and customise them for your brand.

Embedding images based on either a picture upload or a URL. Support jpg, png etc. This would let teams have much more personal dashboards and show images relating to sections in the dashboard.

posts.author: CWithrow (Christina Withrow)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I am trying to create a marketing report to share with the product owners showing them the various marketing campaigns in market, the results, and selections of the creatives. I would love to be able to just embed an image or a video into the dashboard the same way you can in workdocs!

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates or options on adding photos to dashboard not from a link? OR can we put a monday doc on a dashboard?

posts.author: TylerJohnson (Tyler Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like something that should just exist by default. Pretty much any other ‘Dashboard creation’ type of tool would allow for the addition of an image, certain colors, title bars, etc. I would love to have the ability to simply add an image to a Dashboard to make it looks more professional!

posts.author: KKlinger (Kyle Klinger)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any progress with directly uploading photos to the dashboard? This seems like such a basic function.

posts.author: AlinaNZ (Alina Panchenko)
posts.cookedHtml: Our client doesn’t like the look of monday dashboards to share externally, so they are exporting and using the Jotform report… The simple “add image” function will save them and add enough branding to share externally.

posts.author: MaddieRobinson (Maddie Robinson)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that this is a much needed feature - as much as pulling data becomes visual, without using images to be able to show comparisons or make the board more appealing it doesn’t quiet hit the spot

posts.author: Carley (Carley DenBoer)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! I agree that an image widget for the blank views and dashboards would be super helpful. I’d love to add inspirational quotes or encouragements to make my team smile and show my appreciation. I’d also like to display images rather than use an embedded resource because I have a lot of non-techy clients. So, it’s easier for them to understand that they’re looking at a photo, than a tiny portal to another site. For example, instead of embedding a Canva visual, it’s much easier to just look at the downloaded image.

posts.author: brieyost (Brie)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also searching for this feature to add images directly into the dashboard. you can do it in docs, but the width of each section is the width of the whole doc, whereas in the dashboard, widgets have more size customization options. Having both of these capabilities in one place would be ideal.

posts.author: dmvv (Dmitrij Voronov)
posts.cookedHtml:
@brieyost @Carley @MaddieRobinson @KKlinger

Hello,

We at Eobyte are excited to inform you, that our new app Wimage has recently launched in the monday marketplace. Wimage allows you to upload images directly to dashboards.

We are currently somewhat in beta, so for the time being app works only for team members, guests will only see a placeholder.

Don’t hesitate to contact us if you have any useful insight or if you have a specific feature in mind. Hope this helps.

Best regards,
Dmitrij

posts.author: adalew (Ada)
posts.cookedHtml: @dmvv Unfortunately uploaded image (by Wimage) is not visible in exported pdf.

posts.author: ShelleyDonovan (Shelley Donovan Ritchie)
posts.cookedHtml: Where is this in the Monday.com backlog. This is standard, basic and expected in other similar tools. It aggravates Enterprise users to no end when they have to redo dashboard into a work doc to get the images. Why is there stubbornness on this to add this as basic functionality to the dashboard. The time to be better is now, it is a disservice to the reputation of the Monday tool when something is so expected as standard issue ~basic and it exists as ability on the work doc but not on the dashboard. Please advise if there is a standard issue way to get this to occur on the dashboard.
